Motion For Order Establishing Admissions And For Monetary Sanctions And For Terminating Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-27-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Curtis Hagan for defendant and moving party: Randon Mckendrick
Phil Kilduff for plaintiff and opposing party: Zhanna Kleiman

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: The Court has read and considered the untimely opposition filed by counsel for plaintiff. Defendant's motion to deem certain matters admitted is denied as responses have been served. The Court makes no rulings as to the sufficiency of the responses. The motion for terminating sanctions is denied. Plaintiff's failure to respond in a timely fashion was a misuse of the discovery process. Being out of town sporadically over a period of several weeks does not justify failing to respond to properly propounded discovery. However, the Court is unable to determine the appropriate amount of monetary sanctions as the declaration of counsel for defendant does not comply with CCP Section 2023.040.

Plaintiff's request for monetary sanctions is denied. The provisions of CCP 2023.010(i) do not apply as no responses were served prior to the filing of the instant motion. Prevailing party to submit form of order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
